title: senecio covasii
intro: Senecio covasii, also known as Cova's ragwort, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to the Azores Islands. The plant contains pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. Senecio covasii has been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, due to its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Senecio covasii can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support liver health and alleviate symptoms of fever and colds.
